content.title: Eid Mubarak! The Joy of Eid is Here!
content.subtitle: Learn the Sunnah and Rules of Eid Salat
content.body:
We have reached the very last day of Ramadan. Today is both Jumu'ah and the final day of fasting. The time after Asr on Jumu'ah is a highly sought-after window for the acceptance of Dua, and similarly, Duas are accepted just before Iftar. Today, we are blessed with both opportunities simultaneously. Let us pray to Allah for the forgiveness of our sins and those of everyone around us, and seek well-being in this world and the Akhirah. May we celebrate Eid with joy and happiness in a Shariah-compliant manner\!

Rules of Eid Salat (6 Additional Takbirs)

In the 1st Rakat, after reciting Sana, perform 3 extra Takbirs: raise your hands to your ears and leave them at your sides for the 1st and 2nd, but fold them after the 3rd Takbir. The Imam will then complete the Rakat after recitation and Ruku-Sajdah. In the 2nd Rakat, the Imam will perform the recitation first and then give 3 extra Takbirs before moving to Ruku; for all three, raise your hands and leave them at your sides, moving directly to Ruku on the 4th Takbir. It is Wajib to listen attentively to the two Khutbahs delivered by the Imam after the Salat.

Sunnah Acts for the Day of Eid

Various Hadiths describe the practices of the Prophet (SAW) on the day of Eid. Here are several Sunnah-inspired acts summarized:

  1. Reciting Takbir-e-Tashreeq.

  2. Paying Fitra before the Eid Salat.

  3. Waking up early and heading to the Eidgah ahead of time.

  4. Using Miswak, performing Ghusl, and applying perfume (Atar).

  5. Eating an odd number of dates or something sweet before leaving for the Eidgah.

  6. Walking to the Eidgah instead of using a vehicle (if possible).

  7. Changing routes when going to and returning from the Eidgah.

  8. Performing Eid Salat in an open space.

  9. Listening to the Khutbah (Wajib).

  10. Remaining cheerful and greeting others with Eid supplications.

May Allah grant us the ability to celebrate Eid according to the Sunnah. Ameen.

A Final Word...

At Muslims Day, we have strived to support you throughout this entire month of Ramadan. Our Engineering, Design, Content Development, and Support teams have worked well beyond their regular hours for your benefit. We sincerely request your Duas for Muslims Day and Kahf. May Allah accept this project and make every app under Kahf a tool for building a Halal internet ecosystem. We pray that Kahf plays an effective role in protecting you and future generations from the obscenity and Haram of the internet. May Allah grant us all the Tawfiq to continue our work with Ikhlas (sincerity). Ameen.

footer_button.hint: Your feedback and a 5-star rating for the Muslims Day app would mean a lot to us. It motivates our team to keep improving and serving you better.
